# Security Policy

## Supported Versions

The following versions are currently supported with security updates:

| Version | Supported          |
| ------- | ------------------ |
| 1.x     | ✅                 |
| < 1.0   | ❌                 |

## Reporting a Vulnerability

Please **do not** open a public GitHub issue for security vulnerabilities.

### Internal Employees
Report directly through our internal channels:
- 💬 Slack: `#security-incidents`
- 📧 Email: `security@yourcompany.com`

### Contractors & Third Parties
Use one of the following:
- 🔒 GitHub Private Vulnerability Reporting — click **"Report a vulnerability"** in the Security tab
- 📧 Email: `security@yourcompany.com`

## What to Include

Please provide as much of the following as possible:

- **Description** of the vulnerability
- **Steps to reproduce** the issue
- **Potential impact** (data exposure, privilege escalation, etc.)
- **AWS service or resource** involved (if applicable)
- **Suggested fix** (optional)

## What to Expect

| Step | Timeline |
| ---- | -------- |
| Initial response | Within **24 hours** |
| Status update or resolution | Within **7 business days** |
| Coordinated disclosure | Agreed upon with reporter |

## Our Process

1. 🔍 We triage and confirm the report privately
2. 🛠 We develop and test a fix
3. 🚀 We deploy — coordinated with our Security Team
4. 📢 We notify you and credit you (if desired)

We follow a **coordinated disclosure** model — please give us time to patch before going public.

## Policy Reference

This repository is governed by [Company Name]'s internal Vulnerability Disclosure Policy.
> 📄 [Link to Vanta/Confluence policy doc]

## Contact

If you are unsure whether something is a vulnerability, **always err on the side of reporting it.**
We would rather investigate and find nothing than miss a real issue.

**Security Team:** `security@yourcompany.com`
